segments.text: Do you know the time when the mountain goats give birth? Do you watch when the doe bears fawns?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Can you count the months that they fulfill? Or do you know the time when they give birth?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They bow themselves. They bear their young. They end their labor pains.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Their young ones become strong. They grow up in the open field. 
They go out, and don’t return again.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Who has set the wild donkey free? Or who has loosened the bonds of the swift donkey,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: whose home I have made the wilderness, and the salt land his dwelling place?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He scorns the tumult of the city, neither does he hear the shouting of the driver.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The range of the mountains is his pasture. He searches after every green thing.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Will the wild ox be content to serve you? Or will he stay by your feeding trough?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Can you hold the wild ox in the furrow with his harness? Or will he till the valleys after you?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will you trust him, because his strength is great? Or will you leave to him your labor?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will you confide in him, that he will bring home your seed, and gather the grain of your threshing floor?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly, but are they the feathers and plumage of love?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For she leaves her eggs on the earth, warms them in the dust,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: and forgets that the foot may crush them, or that the wild animal may trample them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: She deals harshly with her young ones, as if they were not hers. Though her labor is in vain, she is without fear,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: because God has deprived her of wisdom, neither has he imparted to her understanding.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When she lifts up herself on high, she scorns the horse and his rider.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Have you given the horse might? Have you clothed his neck with a quivering mane?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Have you made him to leap as a locust? The glory of his snorting is awesome.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He paws in the valley, and rejoices in his strength. He goes out to meet the armed men.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He mocks at fear, and is not dismayed, neither does he turn back from the sword.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The quiver rattles against him, the flashing spear and the javelin.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He eats up the ground with fierceness and rage, neither does he st and still at the sound of the trumpet.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
As often as the trumpet sounds he snorts, ‘Aha!’He smells the battle afar off, 
the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Is it by your wisdom that the hawk soars, and stretches her wings toward the south?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up, and makes his nest on high?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: On the cliff he dwells and makes his home, on the point of the cliff and the stronghold.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: From there he spies out the prey. His eyes see it afar off.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His young ones also suck up blood. Where the slain are, there he is.
segments.speaker: The LORD
